# CS — Skill Arm (live)

**Date:** 2026-06-28 · **Faculty:** Science → FB Informatik (+ Tübingen AI Center / ELLIS) · **Arm:** SKILL

## Persona (6 dimensions)

- **Interests:** Machine learning, deep learning, AI (representation learning, probabilistic ML, computer vision)
- **Methods:** Empirical ML (model training/evaluation), some ML theory
- **Domain:** General AI / data science
- **Thesis style:** Experimental / systems ML, empirical
- **Skills:** Python, PyTorch, math/statistics
- **No-gos:** No hardware/embedded systems; no exclusively proof-only theory

## Route (search-strategy §2)

Primary: Science → FB Informatik. Interfaculty leg: Tübingen AI Center / ELLIS Institute / Cluster of Excellence "Machine Learning" / MPI for Intelligent Systems.

## Pass 1 — backbone crawl

- FB Informatik `forschung.html` → research groups list (below).
- Tübingen AI Center / ELLIS Tübingen → cross-faculty ML fellows.

## Option Map (grouped by interest dimension)

### Core machine learning & theory

**1. Maschinelles Lernen — Prof. Dr. Matthias Hein** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: Core ML — robustness, adversarial examples, optimization. Direct hit.
- Pros/difficulties: High-profile, competitive; strong theory+empirical balance.
- Conversation starter: Master's on certified robustness of deep classifiers.

**2. Methoden des maschinellen Lernens — Prof. Dr. Philipp Hennig** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: Probabilistic ML, Bayesian methods, "algorithms as learning machines."
- Pros/difficulties: Strong for probabilistic/numerics-flavored thesis.
- Conversation starter: Probabilistic numerics for uncertainty in deep models.

**3. Theorie des maschinellen Lernens — Prof. Dr. Ulrike von Luxburg** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: ML theory (clustering, graph-based learning, evaluation). 
- Pros/difficulties: Theory-leaning — ⚠ possible partial conflict with "no proof-only"; she also does empirical evaluation work, so confirm project type.
- Conversation starter: Empirical study of embedding/clustering evaluation.

**4. Foundations of Machine Learning Systems — Prof. Dr. Robert C. Williamson** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: Foundations of ML systems, fairness, risk.
- Conversation starter: Master's on fairness/risk measures in ML systems.

**5. Scalable Trustworthy AI (STAI) — Prof. Dr. Seong Joon Oh** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: Trustworthy AI, uncertainty, data-centric ML — strongly empirical deep learning.
- Conversation starter: Uncertainty estimation / data attribution in deep nets.

### ML for science / probabilistic modeling

**6. Maschinelles Lernen in der Wissenschaft — Prof. Dr. Jakob Macke** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: Simulation-based inference, ML for neuroscience/science.
- Conversation starter: Simulation-based inference applied to a scientific model.

**7. Artificial Scientist Lab (ML in Science II) — Prof. Dr. Mario Krenn** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: ML for scientific discovery.
- Conversation starter: ML-driven hypothesis/design generation.

### Computer vision / multimodal deep learning

**8. Autonomes maschinelles Sehen (Autonomous Vision) — Prof. Dr.-Ing. Andreas Geiger** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: Computer vision ∩ ML (3D, neural scene representations, autonomous driving).
- Pros/difficulties: Top CV group; very competitive; some robotics adjacency (but software/vision, not hardware — passes no-go).
- Conversation starter: Neural implicit 3D reconstruction thesis.

**9. Multimodal Learning (Continual Learning on multimodal streams) — Prof. Dr. Gerard Pons-Moll** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: Deep learning for 3D human/body modeling, multimodal.
- Conversation starter: Learning 3D human shape from images.

**10. Neuronale Informationsverarbeitung — Prof. Felix Wichmann** ([uni-tuebingen.de](https://uni-tuebingen.de/fakultaeten/mathematisch-naturwissenschaftliche-fakultaet/fachbereiche/informatik/forschung.html))
- Relevance: ML + human vision, robustness/comparison of DNNs to human perception.
- Conversation starter: Comparing deep-net and human image classification.

### Interfaculty AI Center / ELLIS (cross-listed, deduplicated)

- **Matthias Bethge** (neural/representation learning, robustness), **Zeynep Akata** (multimodal/explainable ML), **Philipp Berens** (ML for neuroscience/medical imaging) — ELLIS Tübingen / AI Center fellows; several also hold Informatik or Medicine affiliations. Strong ML supervisors beyond the core Informatik list.

### Adjacent (surfaced, secondary)

- Computergrafik — Prof. Hendrik Lensch; Kognitive Modellierung — Prof. Martin Butz (cognitive modeling, RL); Applied/Algorithmic Bioinformatics (Kohlbacher, Huson) — ML-adjacent but domain-specific.

> **Coverage caveat:** Covers publicly visible chairs as of 2026-06-28. Chairs with weak web presence may be missing. To catch them: visit the FB Informatik research page and the Tübingen AI Center / ELLIS directories directly, ask the Fachschaft, and check the Vorlesungsverzeichnis.
